primary_accounting_issue: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on intragroup dividend receivable in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs define hedged items and specify the conditions under which foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items may be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs explain the treatment of exchange differences on monetary items that form part of a net investment in a foreign operation and the requirement to recognise them in profit or loss or OCI.

This Interpretation clarifies the nature of the hedged risk and the amount of the hedged item for net investment hedges, and confirms that foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item may qualify as a hedged risk.

peripheral_authority.reason: These disclosures relate to interests in subsidiaries, but do not address hedge accounting treatment for intragroup dividend receivables.
This standard deals with related party disclosures, which is not relevant to the hedge accounting treatment of FX risk on intragroup dividends.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on intragroup dividend receivable

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 para 6.3.6 permits designation of the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item as a hedged item in a net investment hedge in consolidated financial statements, provided the FX gains/losses are not eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9, an entity may designate the foreign currency risk of a financial asset (the dividend receivable) as the hedged risk in a fair value hedge, offsetting fair value changes due to FX movements in profit or loss.
